findings.title: JSDoc claims to 'mirror' context-engine types without enforcement
findings.code_snippet:
/**
 * UI-layer types for memory management panels.
 * Mirror the context-engine types for use in browser/renderer.
 */
findings.description: The JSDoc claims these types 'mirror the context-engine types', but there is no import, schema validation, or type-level link to the actual context-engine types. If context-engine types evolve, this file will silently diverge while the comment still claims they are mirrors. This will mislead future maintainers into assuming type parity that does not exist.
findings.suggestion: Either (a) import and re-export the context-engine types with Pick/Omit to enforce the mirror relationship, or (b) reword the comment to 'Standalone UI-layer types for memory management panels, inspired by context-engine domain types' to avoid implying a live mirror.

findings.title: No JSDoc on exported useMemoryModuleStore or its public methods
findings.description: The project convention (per CLAUDE.md and established codebase patterns) is JSDoc on all exported interfaces and functions. This store exports 7 public methods (setMemories, deleteMemory, selectMemory, clearSelection, resetState) and 4 reactive properties with zero documentation. A future maintainer will not know the intended contract (e.g., does deleteMemory also clear selection if the deleted item was selected?).
findings.suggestion: Add at minimum a store-level JSDoc describing purpose, and JSDoc on non-obvious methods like deleteMemory (which has a side-effect on selectedMemory) and selectMemory.

findings.title: No JSDoc on exported useTodoModuleStore or its public methods
findings.description: Same missing-JSDoc issue. The store has 6 public methods and 2 computed properties with no documentation. Methods like addTodo silently discard empty/whitespace-only input (a behavior worth documenting), and toggleTodo has a timestamp side-effect on completedAt.
findings.suggestion: Add JSDoc to the store and its public methods, especially addTodo (documents the trim + empty-reject behavior) and toggleTodo (documents the completedAt timestamp toggling).

findings.title: No JSDoc on exported useSkillsModuleStore or its public methods
findings.description: Store exports 4 public methods and 2 computed properties with no JSDoc. The filteredSkills computed searches across name, description, and tags -- this multi-field search logic is non-obvious and worth documenting.
findings.suggestion: Add JSDoc, particularly on filteredSkills (documenting the multi-field search across name/description/tags) and toggleSkill.

findings.title: No JSDoc on exported useActivityModuleStore or its public methods
findings.description: Store exports 3 public methods and 3 computed properties with no JSDoc. The activities ref is declared but never consumed by any computed property in this store, which would confuse a maintainer without documentation explaining its intended use.
findings.suggestion: Add JSDoc to the store explaining its role, and document why activities is stored separately from todaySummary (are they consumed differently by the UI?).

findings.title: Template section comments restate visually obvious structure
findings.code_snippet:
<!-- Header with stats -->
<!-- Search and filter -->
<!-- Memory list -->
<!-- Empty state -->
<!-- Detail panel -->
findings.description: All four Vue components use HTML comments like '<!-- Header with stats -->', '<!-- Search and filter -->', '<!-- Memory list -->', etc. to mark template sections. While section markers in long Vue templates can aid navigation, most of these restate what is immediately visually obvious from the template structure (e.g., a v-for over filteredMemories is obviously the memory list). This pattern appears across all 4 components (17 comments total). The maintenance cost is low but so is the value.
findings.suggestion: This is a minor style observation. If the team prefers section markers for template navigation, keep them. Otherwise, consider removing the most obvious ones (e.g., '<!-- Empty state -->' above a v-else block) and keeping only those that mark non-obvious sections.

positive_observations: Zero forbidden patterns (TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX/PLACEHOLDER/TEMP) found across all 9 new files -- clean code discipline.
The types file (memory.ts) has a file-level JSDoc explaining its purpose, which is better than no documentation at all.
Store code is clean and self-documenting with good function names that describe their intent (deleteMemory, toggleTodo, clearCompleted).
Template HTML comments are consistent in style across all 4 Vue components, showing intentional convention.
